body_txt: The hero is in a situation with two unpleasant alternatives and no other options are available to him. Begrudgingly, they hold on to their moral code and choose the lesser of two evils- however vile or loathsome it may appear and manifest before them, will ultimately produce the outcome that minimizes damage and satisfies the absolute majority. Modern elections, especially for the office of President of the United States, are the Trope Codifier for the modern sense of this trope in popular consciousness, and as such many examples are directly based on or otherwise referential to such politics. That being said, many of these are highly subjective and highly contentious, and specific real-life examples should be kept off this page . Compare Black-and-Gray Morality , when those who are technically the "good guys" are the lesser evil, and Enemy Mine , when the good guys work with the lesser evil to defeat a greater one. Even Evil Has Standards may be in play to drive the point home. Closely related to A Lighter Shade of Black , which provides a side to root for. (Those stories usually involve a villain's P.O.V. fighting the other side.) The difference is threefold; if the Lesser of Two Evils is a salient character or an organization or faction, they may bear the same dismal mode of ethics as the "greater evil" yet are a more palatable alternative to the latter due to doing less damage, like taking over the world versus demolishing it , whereas A Lighter Shade of Black considers the morality of the conflicting villains as well. Secondly, as stated above, this trope involves a conscious choice on behalf of the heroes in which they embrace the option with the idea that it will cause less harm then the alternative- if a "lesser of two evils" scenario is implied by the narrative and left for the viewer to decide upon, then it may be a case of A Lighter Shade of Black . Lastly the Lesser of Two Evils may not be a person; for example, in a Mercy Kill scenario, the lesser evil may instead be euthanization versus a Fate Worse than Death . A Sympathetic Villain, Despicable Villain situation might be conceptualized as the natural predecessor to this particular scenario, wherein the circumstances are such that two conflicting villainous factions, or a Big Bad Duumvirate , whence one villain is less destructive/less unscrupulous than the other. Compare and contrast Morton's Fork , when the hero is given the illusion of free choice and both outcomes are equally bad, and contrast wholesale with Failure Is the Only Option , where the hero is granted no opportunity to select even a lesser evil.
